content.explanation:
🌐 Connecting the Web Together

Up to now, you’ve been building standalone pages. But the real power of the internet comes from connections between pages.

This is done using the <a> (anchor) tag, which creates clickable links.

🔗 What is a Link?

A link allows users to jump from one page to another — either:

  • 📄 Another page on your website
  • 🌍 A different website (like Google)
  • 📍 A section inside the same page

This is what makes the web a “web” — everything is connected.
content.syntax_breakdown:
🧪 Anchor Tag Structure

The basic link format is:

<a href="URL">Click Text</a>

📌 Example

<a href="https://example.com">Visit Example Website</a>

The href attribute tells the browser where to go.

🧠 Key Idea

👉 Without href, the link does nothing
👉 The text inside the tag is what users click

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
My Learning Hub

Welcome to my first interactive web page.

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Create your first link 1. Add a new

below the existing paragraph 2. Inside it, create an tag 3. Link text must be: Visit Cymor Tech Services 4. Use this URL: https://cymortech.com 👉 Then run your code and click the link
mini_challenge.hint: 👉 Example format: Visit Cymor Tech Services Make sure href is written correctly and tag is closed properly.

quiz_engine.question: What is the purpose of the <a> tag in HTML?
options: To add images to a webpage
To create clickable links between pages or websites
To format text as bold or italic
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 The <a> tag (anchor tag) is used to create links that connect pages across the web.

takeaways: The <a> tag is used to create hyperlinks
href defines the destination of the link
Links can connect pages, websites, or sections
The internet is built on interconnected links

cheat_sheet.link_text: Visible clickable content inside the tag
